mechanism_narrative: A negative trailing trend in housing starts signals weakening household balance sheets and construction demand; when HOUST is falling, equity earnings expectations are revised down and S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 1032.0 for 2008-04-01, indicating depressed residential construction before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if falling HOUST trends are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment reduces expected household income and raises recession probability; when UNRATE crosses upward by at least 0.4 percentage points over three months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.4pp over the prior 3 months or crosses above 5.5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value was 5.5 for 2008-05-01 before the decision date
regime_hint: labor-market deterioration before or during recession
failure_condition: Reject if the average 20-day S&P 500 return after the UNRATE trigger is not below the unconditional average by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Emergency monetary easing reflects deteriorating credit and growth conditions; when FEDFUNDS falls below 2.25% after a rapid easing cycle, the growth shock dominates discount-rate support and 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 2.25% and has declined by >= 100bp over the prior 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 1.98 for 2008-05-01 before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return exceeds the sample mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with falling policy rates signals a stagflationary constraint: inflation limits further easing while real activity weakens, causing lower equity valuation multiples over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises over 6 months while FEDFUNDS falls over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 215.132 and FEDFUNDS was 1.98 using data available before 2008-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-down interaction has no negative incremental effect versus either single-series signal in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth supported by noncollapsing payrolls indicates resilient nominal consumption and near-term revenue support; when RSAFS rises while PAYEMS is flat or rising, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 385448.0 and PAYEMS vintage value was 137754.0 for 2008-05-01
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-positive and PAYEMS-nonnegative trigger fails to outperform the unconditional 20-day return hit rate by at least 5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production weakness signals falling factory output and operating leverage pressure; when INDPRO crosses below its trailing 6-month average, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its trailing 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value was 111.1807 for 2008-04-01 before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside crossings are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional negative-return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPI trend increases uncertainty about real purchasing power and future policy constraints; when CPIAUCSL momentum is positive, short-horizon S&P 500 returns are negative as inflation risk premia rise.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 215.132 for 2008-05-01 before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive CPIAUCSL momentum has a nonnegative Rank IC with 5-day S&P 500 returns over every rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll contraction reduces aggregate income and corporate sales expectations; when PAYEMS declines over three consecutive monthly releases, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines in 3 consecutive monthly observations
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 137754.0 for 2008-05-01 before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if three-month PAYEMS contraction episodes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward cases.
